perturbation.original_text: (n) "Arizona Trademark License Term" means the period commencing on the Effective Date and ending twenty-four (24) months thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: (n) "Arizona Trademark License Term" means the period commencing on the Effective Date and ending ten (10) years thereafter.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the definition of "Arizona Trademark License Term" from 24 months to 10 years introduces a contradiction with Section 12.1(a), which states the agreement's term regarding Arizona Licensed Trademarks is for the Arizona Trademark License Term. The modification makes the term significantly longer, which could be contested.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.1 Term and Termination. (a) Unless earlier terminated pursuant to the provisions hereof, the term of this Agreement and the licenses and other grants of rights (and related obligations) under this Agreement shall (i) with respect to the Arizona Licensed Trademarks, be for the Arizona Trademark License Term, (ii) with respect to the Diamond Licensed Trademarks, be for the Diamond Trademark License Term, (iii) with respect to the Phase- Out Marks, be for the term set forth in Section 6.6, and (iv) with respect to Copyrights, Know-How and Patents, be in perpetuity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of "Arizona Trademark License Term" from 24 months to 10 years. This directly contradicts Section 12.1(a), which states that the term of the agreement for the Arizona Licensed Trademarks is for the Arizona Trademark License Term. By changing the duration of the defined term, the changed text creates a direct conflict with the provision governing the agreement's duration, making the agreement internally inconsistent and potentially unenforceable. Therefore, the changed text creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6.1 Arizona Licensed Trademark Grant. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Arizona hereby grants to the Company a limited, non- exclusive, royalty-free, non-sublicensable (except as set forth in Section 7.1), non-assignable license in, to and under the Arizona Licensed Trademarks for the Arizona Trademark License Term for use in the Company Field throughout the world only in the form and manner that such Arizona Licensed Trademarks are used in the Business as of the Closing, provided that the Company shall use commercially reasonable efforts to present the Arizona Licensed Trademarks in the form set forth on Schedule 6.1.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.1 Arizona Licensed Trademark Grant. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Arizona hereby grants to the Company a fully exclusive, royalty-free, fully sublicensable and assignable license in, to and under the Arizona Licensed Trademarks for perpetuity for use in any field throughout the universe in any form and manner, and the Company's use of Arizona Licensed Trademarks shall be entirely at Company’s discretion and without the need to adhere to what is stated in Schedule 6.1.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the Arizona Licensed Trademark Grant from 'limited, non-exclusive, non-sublicensable, non-assignable license for the Arizona Trademark License Term' to a 'fully exclusive, fully sublicensable and assignable license for perpetuity' creates a direct conflict with Section 8.1. Section 8.1 states that neither the Company nor its Affiliates will acquire any ownership rights in the Arizona Licensed IP. This modified clause significantly expands the Company’s rights and could be interpreted as de facto ownership, hence the contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.1 Ownership of Arizona Licensed IP. The Buyer Entities acknowledge and agree that (a) Arizona and its Affiliates own the Arizona Licensed IP (other than the Arizona Licensed Trademarks), (b) AWI Licensing LLC owns the Arizona Licensed Trademarks, (b) neither the Company, nor its Affiliates or its Sublicensees, will acquire any ownership rights in the Arizona Licensed IP, and (c) the Company shall not represent or make any claim that it has an ownership interest in any Arizona Licensed IP. Without limitation to the foregoing, the Company shall not file applications to register any Arizona Licensed IP or assist any person in doing the same, or contest, challenge, or otherwise take any action adverse to Arizona's and its Affiliates' ownership of or rights in and to the Arizona Licensed IP, or assist any person in doing the same.
perturbation.justification: The changed text grants the Company a "fully exclusive, fully sublicensable and assignable license for perpetuity," which directly contradicts Section 8.1 stating that "neither the Company, nor its Affiliates or its Sublicensees, will acquire any ownership rights in the Arizona Licensed IP." The expanded rights granted in the changed text, particularly the perpetual term, sublicensability, and assignability, effectively grant the Company rights that are akin to ownership, thus contradicting the explicit denial of ownership rights in Section 8.1.

perturbation.original_text: 9.3 Enforcement. As between the Parties, Arizona, with respect to the Arizona Licensed IP, and the Company or Buyer, with respect to the Company Licensed IP, shall have the right (but not the obligation) to elect to bring a Proceeding or enter into settlement discussions regarding, or otherwise seek to resolve, any infringement, misappropriation, or other violation, or allegations of invalidity or unenforceability, of the Licensed IP. In the event that Arizona declines to institute any Proceedings against third-party infringers or violators of any Arizona Licensed Patents, regarding activities that would fall within the Company Field if conducted by the Company, within forty-five (45) days after being notified or becoming aware of such infringing conduct, the Company or Buyer shall have the right to institute any Proceedings against such third-party infringers or violators. In the event that the Company or Buyer elects to institute such Proceedings, Arizona will reasonably cooperate with the Company or Buyer in such Proceedings, and the Company or Buyer shall reimburse Arizona for all reasonable costs and fees incurred by Arizona as a result of such cooperation. Such cooperation by Arizona will include joining such Proceeding as a party, if deemed necessary by the Company or Buyer. In the event that Arizona elects to bring a Proceeding against any alleged infringer of the Arizona Licensed Trademarks and seeks the cooperation of the Licensor of the Trademark License Agreement in such Proceeding, Arizona will take reasonable steps to assist the Company or Buyer in requesting the cooperation of the Licensor of the Trademark License Agreement, and pursuing an infringement claim against such alleged infringer. The Company or Buyer, as applicable, shall retain all benefits, recoveries, injunctions or other value derived from such Proceedings instituted by such Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.3 Enforcement. As between the Parties, only the Company or Buyer, with respect to the Arizona Licensed IP and Company Licensed IP, shall have the *exclusive* right and *unilateral obligation* to bring a Proceeding or enter into settlement discussions regarding, or otherwise seek to resolve, any infringement, misappropriation, or other violation, or allegations of invalidity or unenforceability, of the Licensed IP. Arizona has no right to involvement in such proceedings. The Company or Buyer, as applicable, shall retain all benefits, recoveries, injunctions or other value derived from such Proceedings instituted by such Party.
perturbation.explanation: By modifying Section 9.3 to grant the Company or Buyer the *exclusive right* and *unilateral obligation* to enforce both Arizona Licensed IP *and* Company Licensed IP, while simultaneously preventing Arizona's involvement, contradicts the initial part of the section, which grants Arizona rights with respect to their own IP. This creates a situation where the Company is *obligated* to defend IP they do not own, and Arizona is denied the ability to protect its own IP. This introduces an in-text contradiction because the enforcement rights are fundamentally altered.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.3 Enforcement. As between the Parties, only the Company or Buyer, with respect to the Arizona Licensed IP and Company Licensed IP, shall have the *exclusive* right and *unilateral obligation* to bring a Proceeding or enter into settlement discussions regarding, or otherwise seek to resolve, any infringement, misappropriation, or other violation, or allegations of invalidity or unenforceability, of the Licensed IP. Arizona has no right to involvement in such proceedings. The Company or Buyer, as applicable, shall retain all benefits, recoveries, injunctions or other value derived from such Proceedings instituted by such Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text grants the Company or Buyer the 'exclusive right and unilateral obligation' to enforce both Arizona Licensed IP and Company Licensed IP and prevents Arizona's involvement, directly contradicting the initial part of the original Section 9.3, which grants Arizona rights with respect to their own IP. The changed text and contradicted text are the same, reinforcing that it is a change to an area in the original text which is now directly contradicted by the modification, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
